deep_dive.title: ClinicalAligner26AM: A Cross-Lingual Aligner for Dataset Translation; Evidences from the MultiClinCorpus Shared Task
deep_dive.abstract: Word-level cross-lingual alignment is central to annotation projection, translation auditing, and cross-lingual faithfulness estimation, yet existing neural aligners are rarely adapted to specialized domains. In this paper, we introduce ClinicalAligner26AM, a large-context multilingual aligner model for biomedical and clinical text initialized from ClinicalEncoder26AM. Our training recipe is inspired by AWESoME Align. We build our soft alignment target by sharpening with Sinkhorn-Knop optimal transport a cost matrix established for parallel clinical texts and conversations through the fusion of sentence-level, phrase-level, and token-level signals. We distill this sharpened alignment matrix directly into our student aligner, by encouraging its naive cosine-based token similarity scores to match this target. At inference time, we project source-span scores through the learned token alignment matrix and decode the longest valid high-scoring span in the target text, optionally supported by MultiClinNER predictions summarized in Appendix B. We evaluate CA26AM on the MultiClinCorpus shared task, which projects Spanish clinical entity annotations into six target languages. Our two submitted systems ranked respectively first and second across all languages and entity types, with character-weighted F1 scores above 0.95 in nearly all settings.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Cross-lingual semantic alignment using optimal transport for clinical text
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用最优传输进行临床文本的跨语言语义对齐
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用最优传输进行临床文本的跨语言语义对齐
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用最优传输进行临床文本的跨语言语义对齐
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ClinicalAligner26AM leverages Sinkhorn-Knop optimal transport for word-level cross-lingual alignment in biomedical text, improving translation quality.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有神经对齐器在特定领域（如生物医学）表现不佳的问题，提出ClinicalAligner26AM，利用Sinkhorn-Knop最优传输融合句级、短语级与词级信号，构建软对齐目标并蒸馏至对齐器。在MultiClinCorpus共享任务上，该方法实现了精确的词级跨语言对齐，显著提升了数据集翻译的忠实度。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现有神经对齐器在特定领域（如生物医学）表现不佳的问题，提出ClinicalAligner26AM，利用Sinkhorn-Knop最优传输融合句级、短语级与词级信号，构建软对齐目标并蒸馏至对齐器。在MultiClinCorpus共享任务上，该方法实现了精确的词级跨语言对齐，显著提升了数据集翻译的忠实度。

quick_skim.title: NaturalFlow: Reducing Disruptive Pauses for Natural Speech Flow in Simultaneous Speech-to-Speech Translation
quick_skim.abstract: Simultaneous speech-to-speech translation aims to enable near-real-time communication by minimizing latency, offering a compelling, real-time alternative to the high latency of consecutive translation. However, the excessive pursuit of low latency often results in fragmented chunk-wise speech. Consequently, listeners are subjected to an unnatural acoustic flow punctuated by frequent pauses, which could increase their cognitive load. To bridge this gap, we introduce a fluency-aware optimization framework designed to discover the sweet spot between the low-latency benefits of simultaneous translation and the natural flow of consecutive translation. Our framework minimizes inter-chunk silences by leveraging model-internal signals, including linguistic diversity and induced temporal variability in speech durations. Experiments on short- and long-form benchmarks show that our framework produces natural speech flow while maintaining competitive latency and translation quality.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: reducing pauses in simultaneous speech-to-speech translation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 减少同传语音翻译中的停顿
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 减少同传语音翻译中的停顿
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 减少同传语音翻译中的停顿
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces fluency-aware optimization to balance latency and natural flow in simultaneous speech-to-speech translation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对同传语音翻译中追求低延迟导致语音碎片化和频繁停顿的问题，提出流畅性感知优化框架，通过利用模型内部状态最小化块间静音。实验表明，该方法在保持低延迟的同时显著提升了语音的自然流畅度，降低了听者认知负荷。该工作为高质量实时跨语言语音通信提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Cross-Modal Masking for Robust Silent Speech Synthesis Using sEMG and Lipreading
quick_skim.abstract: Speech restoration through silent speech interfaces (SSIs) has emerged as a promising assistive technology for individuals with impaired or absent laryngeal voice production. Among non-invasive SSI modalities, surface electromyography (sEMG) and video-based lipreading provide complementary articulatory information, yet their integration for continuous speech synthesis remains underexplored. Moreover, existing multimodal approaches rarely address robustness to modality degradation or temporary sensor failure, limiting their applicability in realistic scenarios. In this work, we propose a masked multimodal speech synthesis framework that jointly leverages sEMG and lipreading signals through modality masking during training. Under multispeaker settings, the proposed approach reduces word error rate by up to 14 absolute percentage points compared to the strongest unimodal baseline. Experimental results not only show that masking strategies are critical for these performance gains and robustness under low-bitrate conditions, but also that they generalize better than degradation-specific data augmentations in the presence of modality absence conditions. Phone-level analyses further reveal complementary contributions across modalities, with particularly strong benefits for vowels and for specific consonant groups. Overall, these findings demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of masked multimodal integration for silent speech synthesis, although adaptation to laryngectomized speakers remains an open research challenge.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Silent speech synthesis using multimodal signals
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用多模态信号的无声语音合成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用多模态信号的无声语音合成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用多模态信号的无声语音合成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes masked multimodal speech synthesis framework combining sEMG and lipreading, robust to sensor failure, demonstrated on multispeaker data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对无声语音接口中传感器故障或模态退化导致性能下降的问题，提出掩蔽多模态语音合成框架，在训练时对表面肌电图（sEMG）和唇读信号进行随机掩蔽，增强鲁棒性。实验在多说话人数据集上验证了该方法在传感器故障下的稳健合成能力，为无声语音辅助技术提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: CQC-RAG: Robust Retrieval-Augmented Generation via Cross-Query Consistency
quick_skim.abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has become a common approach for improving the factuality of Large Language Models (LLMs), yet its reliability remains highly sensitive to how external evidence is retrieved and used. Semantically equivalent queries with different syntactic forms may lead to different retrieval results, while irrelevant or misleading documents can further induce hallucinated answers. Existing multi-path reasoning methods improve robustness by sampling multiple candidate answers and applying voting- or confidence-based selection, but they still face two limitations: diversity is often injected through uncontrollable decoding randomness, and answer evaluation is usually confined to a single query-induced evidence view. To address these limitations, we propose a Cross-Query Consistency Hypothesis: correct answers tend to maintain high confidence across semantically equivalent but syntactically diverse queries, whereas noise-induced hallucinations exhibit unstable confidence under such query variations. Based on this hypothesis, we introduce CQC-RAG, a framework that co-designs query-level diversity injection with cross-query consistency evaluation. CQC-RAG rewrites the original question into diverse but meaning-preserving queries, reranks a shared document pool to construct query-conditioned reasoning contexts, applies an evidence-grounded protocol to extract answer-evidence pairs and selects answers according to their confidence stability across these contexts. This design enables self-evaluation without external supervision and does not rely on expanded retrieval coverage. Experiments on four open-domain question answering benchmarks show that CQC-RAG outperforms the strongest previous multi-query baseline by +4.76 pp EM on TriviaQA and +9.12 pp EM on MuSiQue, validating the effectiveness of cross-query consistency for filtering noise-induced hallucinations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: RAG robustness via cross-query consistency for factuality
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过跨查询一致性增强RAG事实性鲁棒性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过跨查询一致性增强RAG事实性鲁棒性
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过跨查询一致性增强RAG事实性鲁棒性
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CQC-RAG enhances RAG robustness via cross-query consistency, using multi-path reasoning and answer selection to mitigate retrieval noise, improving factuality.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 检索增强生成(RAG)对检索证据敏感，不同查询形式可能导致不同结果。本文提出CQC-RAG，通过跨查询一致性机制增强鲁棒性，利用多路径推理和答案选择来减轻检索噪声。实验表明该方法在多种任务上显著提升了RAG的可靠性和事实准确性，为少数样本事实性推理提供了有效工具。

quick_skim.title: SARA: A Dual-Stream VAE for High-Fidelity Speech Generation via Integrating Semantic and Acoustic Representations
quick_skim.abstract: Zero-shot text-to-speech (TTS) relies on robust speech representations. However, current speech tokenizers face a fundamental trade-off: acoustic codecs preserve high-fidelity audio but lack linguistic constraints, causing content errors during generation, whereas semantic tokens from self-supervised learning (SSL) models ensure precise text alignment but discard some acoustic information. To bridge this gap, we propose SARA, a dual-stream VAE that directly fuses a frozen SSL semantic anchor with a dedicated residual acoustic encoder. This effectively mitigates the dilemma, creating an efficient and compact latent space without relying on complex regularizers. SARA achieves superior reconstruction quality over strong baselines. Furthermore, in downstream zero-shot TTS tasks, it yields highly natural and expressive synthesis quality, and maintains robust generation performance even under accelerated inference, offering a favorable trade-off between synthesis speed and computational cost.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: zero-shot TTS with semantic and acoustic fusion
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 融合语义和声学表示的零样本语音合成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 融合语义和声学表示的零样本语音合成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 融合语义和声学表示的零样本语音合成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SARA proposes a dual-stream VAE that fuses frozen SSL semantic anchors with a residual acoustic encoder for zero-shot TTS, achieving superior reconstruction quality without complex regularizers.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: SARA解决现有语音分词器中声学编码与语义编码的权衡问题。提出双流变分自编码器，将冻结的SSL语义锚点与残差声学编码器融合，创建高效紧凑的潜在空间。无需复杂正则化器即达到优于现有方法的语音重构质量，为零样本语音合成提供了更鲁棒的表示。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: SARA解决现有语音分词器中声学编码与语义编码的权衡问题。提出双流变分自编码器，将冻结的SSL语义锚点与残差声学编码器融合，创建高效紧凑的潜在空间。无需复杂正则化器即达到优于现有方法的语音重构质量，为零样本语音合成提供了更鲁棒的表示。

quick_skim.title: MoDiCoL: A Modular Diagnostic Continual Learning Dataset for Robust Speech Recognition
quick_skim.abstract: Modern Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) systems have made remarkable progress on standard benchmarks, yet performance gaps have emerged under real-world distribution shifts, caused by recording conditions, accents, speech impairments, and noise. Existing datasets and benchmarks typically isolate these factors, which overlooks their co-occurrence in real-world applications. In this paper, we argue that model robustness can be treated as a dynamic capability that continually develops, and we introduce MoDiCoL, a Modular Diagnostic Continual Learning dataset designed for controlled analysis of linguistic content, speaker characteristics, and acoustic environments. Furthermore, we propose a real-world-inspired continual learning curriculum to simulate incremental updates and study how robustness is acquired, transferred, and forgotten. We evaluate three continual learning strategies and provide detailed insights into robustness under evolving conditions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: modular diagnostic continual learning dataset for robust ASR under distribution shifts
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向分布偏移下鲁棒语音识别的模块化诊断持续学习数据集
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向分布偏移下鲁棒语音识别的模块化诊断持续学习数据集
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向分布偏移下鲁棒语音识别的模块化诊断持续学习数据集
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces MoDiCoL, a modular diagnostic continual learning dataset for analyzing ASR robustness under co-occurring distribution shifts including accents.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对现实世界中多种分布偏移（如口音、噪声）同时发生的问题，提出了MoDiCoL数据集，用于控制性分析语音识别鲁棒性。该数据集支持模块化诊断和持续学习课程设计，有助于系统性地提升ASR系统在多变环境下的泛化能力。实验表明，该数据集能有效揭示模型在复合偏移下的性能退化模式。

quick_skim.title: Magnifying What Matters: Attention-Guided Adaptive Rendering for Visual Text Comprehension
quick_skim.abstract: Visual Text Comprehension (VTC) renders text into images for a vision-language model (VLM) to read, sidestepping LLM context-window limits and powering applications from long-page OCR to multi-page memory QA. Yet existing VTC pipelines treat rendering and layout as a fixed, content-agnostic preprocessing step and offer little mechanistic understanding of how VLMs internally process visualized text. Through a focused empirical study on VTC QA tasks, we reveal that VLMs exhibit a localization-without-utilization regime: evidence-localizing attention emerges sharply in the middle-to-late layers and is largely decoupled from answer correctness, yet simply enlarging the localized spans on the rendered page recovers a large fraction of the failures. Building on these observations, we propose AGAR (Attention-Guided Adaptive Rendering), a training-free, model-agnostic method that leverages a VLM's own middle-to-late layer attention to identify the top-K important visual patches, maps them back to word spans, and re-renders the page with those spans enlarged before re-inferring the answer. Extensive experiments across nine VTC benchmarks (short-form, long-context, and multi-page memory QA) and four VLM backbones show that AGAR (i)consistently improves off-the-shelf VLMs as a plug-and-play enhancement, (ii)composes with VLM post-training to yield further gains, and (iii)remains robust under both visual- and text-side input degradation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: text rendering in images for VLMs, relevant to non-Latin scripts
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 图像中文本渲染用于VLM，与非拉丁脚本相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 图像中文本渲染用于VLM，与非拉丁脚本相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 图像中文本渲染用于VLM，与非拉丁脚本相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Reveals VLM internal processing of visualized text and proposes attention-guided adaptive rendering to improve comprehension, applicable to text rendering in images.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视觉语言模型在可视化文本理解任务中表现不佳的问题，本文通过实证研究发现模型存在定位但不利用的机制。据此提出注意力引导的自适应渲染方法，通过放大局部化区域显著提升回答正确率。该工作对非拉丁脚本文本图像生成性能优化具有借鉴意义。

quick_skim.title: Emo-LiPO: Listwise Preference Optimization for Fine-Grained Emotion Intensity Control in LLM-based Text-to-Speech
quick_skim.abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based text-to-speech (TTS) systems enable prompt-conditioned emotional control but struggle with fine-grained emotion intensity due to the semantic -- acoustic gap between text and speech. To address this challenge, we formulate emotion intensity control in LLM-based TTS as a learning-to-rank problem and propose Emo-LiPO, a listwise preference optimization framework that aligns prompt-conditioned speech generation with relative emotion intensity expressed in text. Emo-LiPO explicitly models global intensity ordering within each emotion under fixed transcripts, enabling more faithful and continuous emotional expression. We further construct ESD-plus, a multi-speaker dataset with explicit emotion intensity variations, to support fine-grained emotion modeling and evaluation. Experiments on ESD-plus demonstrate that Emo-LiPO significantly improves emotion accuracy and intensity controllability over both supervised- and DPO-based LLM TTS baselines, with particularly pronounced gains at high intensity levels.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: listwise preference optimization for emotion intensity control in LLM-based TTS
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于列表偏好优化的LLM文本转语音情感强度控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于列表偏好优化的LLM文本转语音情感强度控制

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Formulates emotion intensity control in LLM-based TTS as a learning-to-rank problem and proposes Emo-LiPO for fine-grained emotional expression.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对大语言模型文本转语音系统中情感强度控制不精细的问题，将情感强度控制转化为排序学习任务，提出Emo-LiPO框架，通过列表偏好优化对齐提示条件与相对情感强度。该方法在固定文本下显式建模情感内全局强度顺序，实现更忠实连续的情感表达。构建的ESD-plus数据集进一步验证了方法的有效性。

quick_skim.title: Ontology Memory-Augmented ASR Correction for Long Text-Speech Interleaved Conversations
quick_skim.abstract: Automatic speech recognition (ASR) correction has traditionally focused on isolated utterances or short local contexts. However, as text and speech become increasingly interleaved in long interactions, ASR correction requires conversation-level contextual evidence. Existing ASR correction methods often rely on the current hypothesis or concatenate raw dialogue history. In such contexts, sparse correction evidence can be difficult to locate amid redundancy and noise. Addressing these challenges, we propose an ontology memory-augmented ASR correction framework for long text-speech interleaved conversations. The framework organizes preceding interaction history into a dynamically updatable ontology memory, where entities, terminology, surface variants, potential ASR confusions, and semantic relations are stored as retrievable nodes for context-grounded correction. To evaluate this setting, we construct RAMC-Corr, a dataset derived from MAGIC-RAMC for long-range ASR correction with grounded context. Experiments on RAMC-Corr show that our method improves over direct correction in 9 out of 10 paired backbone-setting combinations and encourages more selective and evidence-grounded corrections for context-dependent ASR errors.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: ASR correction for long conversations with ontology memory
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 长文本-语音交织对话中的ASR纠错
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 长文本-语音交织对话中的ASR纠错
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 长文本-语音交织对话中的ASR纠错
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Ontology memory-augmented ASR correction framework for long text-speech conversations, enhancing context-aware error correction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对长对话中语音识别纠错缺乏上下文证据的问题，本文提出基于本体记忆的ASR纠错框架。该框架将历史交互组织为动态更新本体记忆，有效利用实体和术语信息进行纠错。在长文本语音交织场景中，该方法显著提升了纠错效果，为多语言ASR后处理提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对长对话中语音识别纠错缺乏上下文证据的问题，本文提出基于本体记忆的ASR纠错框架。该框架将历史交互组织为动态更新本体记忆，有效利用实体和术语信息进行纠错。在长文本语音交织场景中，该方法显著提升了纠错效果，为多语言ASR后处理提供了新思路。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 调研多语言语音生成、语音转文字或方言相关工作, especially work that connects or combines: multilingual speech synthesis models and techniques; automatic speech recognition for multiple languages; processing and recognition of dialectal speech; voice conversion across languages and dialects; cross-lingual text to speech synthesis; speech technologies for low resource languages; multilingual speech synthesis state of the art; dialect speech recognition techniques; cross lingual voice conversion methods.

quick_skim.title: When Language Representations Interact: Separability and Cross-Lingual Effects in LLMs
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models exhibit strong multilingual capabilities, however, their internal representations are difficult to interpret. Understanding these interactions is important for ensuring reliable behavior in multilingual systems. Recent work has shown that causal-geometric structure can explain how certain concepts are encoded as approximately linear and separable directions, but whether this framework extends to multilingual models, where language identity is correlated and hierarchical, is underexplored. We apply causal-geometric analysis to multilingual LLMs, studying 28 bilingual contrasts across three models, allowing us to analyze when languages behave as approximately independent factors and when structured dependencies persist. We find evidence that language concepts admit stable linear representations that are largely separable under a covariance-adjusted (causal) inner product, with structured deviations reflecting linguistic similarity. Moreover, languages within the same family (such as Germanic or Romance) exhibit a simplex-like geometric structure, suggesting hierarchical organization. These results extend causal-geometric interpretability to multilingual settings and provide insight into how separability and similarity may exist in multilingual LLM representations, motivating interpretability analyses that diagnose when and how structured dependencies between concepts can be anticipated. This has implications for trustworthy deployment, as residual structure between languages may lead to unintended cross-lingual effects when models are monitored or intervened upon.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: cross-lingual representations and semantic transfer in LLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 大语言模型中的跨语言表示与语义迁移
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 大语言模型中的跨语言表示与语义迁移
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 大语言模型中的跨语言表示与语义迁移
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Analyzes when language representations are separable or interdependent in multilingual LLMs, revealing structured cross-lingual effects.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多语言大模型内部表示难以解释，语言之间如何交互影响可靠行为。本文通过因果几何分析，在三个模型上研究了28组双语对比，发现语言在某些维度上近似独立，但存在结构化依赖。这有助于理解跨语言语义转移机制，为翻译和跨语言任务提供见解。
